Investigate Integration of Review Occasion Data into Shopify (Search & PDP Enhancements)
entries.title: Investigate Integration of Review Occasion Data into Shopify (Search & PDP Enhancements)
entries.description:
As part of enhancing the product discovery and gifting experience, there is a recommendation to surface product review insights that indicate the percentage of customers purchasing products for specific occasions (e.g. birthdays, anniversaries).

Additionally, we want to explore whether this “occasion” data can be fed into the Shopify backend to improve search functionality and enable more relevant gift-based discovery.

This task is to investigate the feasibility of capturing, storing, and leveraging review-based occasion data within Shopify.

Scope of Work (Investigation):

  • Review current product review platform capabilities (e.g. does it capture occasion-based data?)

  • Assess how review data (occasion tags/percentages) can be accessed (API/export)

  • Investigate options to:

    • Store occasion data in Shopify (e.g. metafields, tags, metaobjects)

    • Sync data between review platform and Shopify

  • Evaluate how this data could be used:

    • PDP display (e.g. “X% bought for birthdays”)

    • Shopify search filtering / boosting (e.g. “gift for birthday”)

  • Identify any platform limitations (Shopify native search vs third-party search tools)

entries.note: Sprint 12: MTO PDP - Client Feedback Final Round
entries.title: Sprint 12: MTO PDP - Client Feedback Final Round
Creative Concepts
entries.description:
Overview

The Creative Concepts bring to life the visual look and feel of the solution, aligning the brand’s identity with the experience we are designing. Using insights gathered during Discovery – and specifically the Creative Workshop – we create a set of design concepts that define how the solution will look and feel in practice.

This step sets the foundation for the entire design phase, ensuring that the visual direction is fully aligned with the brand’s DNA before moving into full template and component design.

Further information

Actions for Delivery Lead

  • Schedule in internal kickoff to onboard Designer

  • Schedule internal review time & internal feedback

entries.title: Design Rollout
entries.description:
Overview

The Design Rollout takes the approved Creative Concepts and applies them across the complete site experience. Every page template, component, and interaction pattern is designed to ensure a consistent, user-focused, and brand-aligned experience.

This stage also produces a comprehensive style guide that defines the visual language and provides the development team with the assets and specifications needed to begin building the site.

Further information

Actions for Delivery Lead

  • DL to schedule internal meeting with TL prior to presenting to client, to ensure designs are achievable and validate designs

  • Schedule client call to present design rollout

entries.note: Runaway the Label — Commerce Turnaround (Emergency Diagnostic)
entries.title: Runaway the Label — Commerce Turnaround (Emergency Diagnostic)
entries.description:
Service: UX Strategy / Senior UX Strategist

Description:

As the first phase of Runaway the Label's engagement, we need to deliver a Commerce Turnaround — an emergency diagnostic to identify areas of underperformance across the site before we move into the platform migration. This has been sold as a one-week program and needs to be resourced and started as soon as possible.

Scope of work

Emergency Diagnostic Review the current site and identify all key areas of underperformance. This should cover both experience and technical — a UX/strategy lens on what's broken or underdelivering, as well as a technical review of Core Web Vitals and site health. Output should be a clearly presented document or deck summarising findings.

Root Cause Analysis For each area of underperformance identified, document the likely root cause and flag whether it can be addressed within the platform migration or needs to be scoped as a separate piece of work.

Performance Accountability — Recovery KPI Setup Establish a baseline of today's key metrics (conversion rate, Core Web Vitals, etc.) and define where we want to take them. These KPIs will be used to measure the success of the migration and will form the basis of ongoing strategic conversations with the client post-launch.

Suggested resource Kate or Dave (UX Strategy lead). AI tooling should be leveraged where possible to support the analysis and speed up output production.

Timeline ASAP — client was told work would begin within one week of sign-off. Confirm start date with Han by end of day.

Notes

  • This feeds directly into the platform migration (six sprints, ~$49k) which follows

  • The client is also on a $5k support pack — Kat is already using this to help review ecommerce manager candidates

  • A post-launch review (~30 days from go-live) will check performance against the KPIs set in this diagnostic — to be confirmed whether this sits in the project budget or support pack

Attached the sales dec for reference

entries.note: Audience Test – Import Customer List into Autumn Appeal PMAX
entries.title: Audience Test – Import Customer List into Autumn Appeal PMAX
entries.description:
Test whether using a direct customer list upload (1st-party audience) improves campaign performance compared with the current GA4 cookie-based audience signals.

Actions

  1. Extract available donor/customer list from CRM or fundraising platform.

  2. Clean and format data for Google Ads Customer Match upload.

  3. Upload audience list into Google Ads.

  4. Add audience as a signal to the Autumn Appeal Performance Max campaign.

  5. Monitor performance vs current GA4 audience signals.

Metrics to Review

  • ROAS

  • Conversion value

  • Conversion rate

  • Average donation value

Outcome

Determine whether 1st-party data targeting improves performance, informing Winter Appeal targeting strategy.

  2. Evaluate potential campaign structures:

    • National vs state campaigns

    • Audience targeting approach

Outcome

entries.title: APG Design System Brief
entries.description:
Context
We previously completed the JAG website redesign, which now serves as a strong foundation in both design and technical architecture. Given APG also manages Sportscraft and Saba, we would like to leverage the existing JAG theme and structure to create efficiencies across brands, rather than designing each site from scratch.


To enable this, we need to extract and simplify the JAG design system by removing brand-specific elements, creating a neutral base that can be re-skinned. Each brand will then apply its own identity with minor variations (approx. 5–10%), while maintaining consistency in core components and functionality.
A work order is already prepared and ready to proceed, pending any budget confirmation if required.

Deliverables

  • Duplicated JAG design system with all brand-specific styling removed

  • Clean, neutral (vanilla) base design system aligned to existing JAG theme and architecture

  • APG-branded design system applied to the base

  • Defined framework to allow 5–10% visual customisation per brand (JAG, Sportscraft, Saba)

  • Reusable components and patterns to support rollout across multiple APG brand

entries.title: Rip Curl - Deep Dive Analysis
entries.description:
Files:

  • Google Sheet working file

  • Deep Dive report

  • Oscar’s Tech Audit

  • Rip Curl shared priority & roadmap - Jas has requested we update “Sales Findings” tab

__

Overview
Validate whether performance is actually down ~30% (EU) post Shopify launch vs Magento, and identify where/why.

  • Sales reported 30% down but no validation - GA4 tracking completely unreliable due to:

    • Server-side tracking on Magento vs browser-side on Shopify

    • Consent tracking issues since launch

    • Multiple tracking bugs and implementation problems

  • Launch dates:

    • AU/NZ launched 14th Oct 2025

    • EU/US launched 5th Nov 2025

    • Other markets launched month intervals after

    • Multiple launch phases created data complexity

Scope

  • Sales by country (Magento vs Shopify)

    • Exclude EU countries no longer serviced

    • Check currency accuracy + returns impact

  • Organic performance by country

    • GSC + Merchant Centre (traffic, clicks, visibility)

  • Paid performance by country

    • Google Ads (traffic, CVR, revenue)

  • Sanity check tracking + reporting consistency

Output

  • Confirm if 30% drop is valid

  • Where the drop is occurring

  • Key drivers / issues

Dependencies

  • Required data:

    1. Historical Magento sales data by country

    2. Historical Magento sales data by category (if product categories consistent between platforms)

  • Marketing promo calendar requested for qualitative overlay
